rationale: In the related literature discussion, the authors do more than list this paper; they explicitly summarize Kivetz et al. (2006) as showing goal-gradient behavior, where effort increases as agents get closer to a goal. They use that behavioral goal-attainment account as one of the main existing explanations for observed dynamic effort patterns. The citing paper then positions its own contribution as a distinct, contract-theoretic explanation, so this citation materially informs framing and positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Kivetz et al. (2006) as a prior behavioral explanation of increasing effort near goals and contrasts it with its own optimal-contracting mechanism for similar effort profiles. The role is conceptual positioning via contrast, not direct method or model extension.
